body: ``` /codex https://github.com/xlabtg/Teleton-Client/pull/2 —think max ``` ``` ❌ A working session is already running for this URL. URL: https://github.com/xlabtg/Teleton-Client/pull/2 Session: 77cf7b88-2fbf-48ef-85f2-7c2b2ce00763 💡 Wait for the current session to complete, or use /solve_stop to cancel it. ``` ``` box@6ff1ed98ca63:~$ $ --status 77cf7b88-2fbf-48ef-85f2-7c2b2ce00763 ac33ba27-b642-4b88-889d-bc562aa184ce uuid ac33ba27-b642-4b88-889d-bc562aa184ce status executed exitCode 1 command "solve https://github.com/xlabtg/Teleton-Client/pull/2 --think max --tool codex --attach-logs --verbose --no-tool-check --auto-accept-invite --tokens-budget-stats --auto-attach-solution-summary" logPath /tmp/start-command/logs/isolation/screen/ac33ba27-b642-4b88-889d-bc562aa184ce.log startTime "2026-04-24T15:18:18.154Z" endTime "2026-04-24T17:38:32.324Z" workingDirectory /home/box shell /bin/sh platform linux options isolated screen isolationMode detached sessionName 77cf7b88-2fbf-48ef-85f2-7c2b2ce00763 user false keepAlive false useCommandStream false ``` The task working session was finished, but /codex command was not allowing to execute the continuation of pull request. With `--isolation screen` enabled, we don't actually need any timeout or timing interval to safe guard repeated execution. We can actually check the status of task, and in this case it was `status executed`. ``` /codex https://github.com/xlabtg/Teleton-Client/pull/2 —think max ``` ``` ✅ Solve command started successfully! 📊 Session: 77cf7b88-2fbf-48ef-85f2-7c2b2ce00763 🔒 Isolation: screen Requested by: @anton\_poroshin URL: https://github.com/xlabtg/Teleton-Client/pull/2 🛠 Options: --think max --tool codex 🔒 Locked options: --attach-logs --verbose --no-tool-check --auto-accept-invite --tokens-budget-stats --auto-attach-solution-summary --isolation screen 🔔 You will receive a notification when the session finishes. ``` And previous message was not updated with actual status up on execution. It states `Solve command started successfully!`, instead of `command executed`, and in that case the result was non zero, so we should treat it as fail. So execution failed. Also as now we have reliable way to check status of commands under `--isolation screen`, we should show emoji that displays progress, and show `Executing...` until it actually finished with success or fail. This is how executing status looks like: ``` box@6ff1ed98ca63:~$ $ --status c498f8db-5ea7-4cc9-96e2-e88c50f2109a d081fe37-4b46-44f9-b3ec-41e22e865995 uuid d081fe37-4b46-44f9-b3ec-41e22e865995 status executing command "solve https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/issues/104 --think max --tool codex --attach-logs --verbose --no-tool-check --auto-accept-invite --tokens-budget-stats --auto-attach-solution-summary" logPath /tmp/start-command/logs/isolation/screen/d081fe37-4b46-44f9-b3ec-41e22e865995.log startTime "2026-04-24T17:35:30.970Z" currentTime "2026-04-24T17:44:26.028Z" workingDirectory /home/box shell /bin/sh platform linux options isolated screen isolationMode detached sessionName c498f8db-5ea7-4cc9-96e2-e88c50f2109a user false keepAlive false useCommandStream false box@6ff1ed98ca63:~$ ``` We can also use startTime and endTime, to actually calculate how long the task was executing (fail or success). Like failed in 10 minutes and 31 seconds, or succeeded in 15 minutes and 45 seconds. We also should double check the /limits command and queue, and all other places where we track status. So now we can keep UUIDs for `--isolation screen`, and calculate actual number of ``` Queues claude (pending: 0, processing: 0) agent (pending: 0, processing: 0) codex (pending: 0, processing: 1) ``` We should keep previous fallback for claude/agent/codex processes, but we should only use maximum: either we show all `$ --status` executing count, for example 4, or if we don't have that data, or it is lower than number of CLI processes - we show 2 codex/claude/agent. Remember we should support `--isolation screen` and regular flow at the same time, so usually each screen will have one codex/claude/agent, but also solve process itself can be in monitoring mode, so number of executing screens may be more than actual AI CLI processes. So in queue processing we show maximum of `--isolation screen` count or AI CLI processes. So queue can correctly function at all times, and use these values to restrict access when not enough resources (as per existing logic). Now we change only how we calculate number of executing tasks. Please double check all other related places, and be very careful with all your changes, everything that can be tested should be unit tested and integration tested if possible. We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions). If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration. If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.
title: Fix solve completion status monitoring in all places
